reviews.review_title: Feature Request from a Young Creator
reviews.review_description:
Hi there! My son is a huge fan of Grab and has been building in the editor every day. He asked me to pass along a feature request that would make an enormous difference for young creators like him.

Right now, if players want a main menu, a loading screen, or a cut scene, the only option is to place the player in a small boxed-in space and use animation triggers to fake the effect before teleporting them to another map. It works, but it feels limiting.

His dream is to have official tools inside the editor for:

• Making main menus
• Adding loading screens
• Creating real cutscenes
• Placing UI elements that “glue” to where the player is looking (like a true HUD), instead of only existing as world objects

These tools would give creators a way to make polished, story-driven experiences without all the workaround tricks. He’s really passionate about building full games inside Grab, and features like these would help him and a lot of other kids bring their ideas to life.

Thank you for making such an inspiring platform. My boy absolutely loves it, and I am in awe of the creativity and innovation he is able to articulate.

I also love the community, sometimes it’s a little bad, but it’s very nice and welcoming, especially when I first got Grab.

reviews.review_title: I LOVE THIS GAME ITS MY FAVORITE
reviews.review_description: Grab VR is honestly one of the most fun free VR games I’ve played. The movement feels smooth, and the community-made maps give you almost endless things to do. Whether you’re climbing giant structures, speedrunning obstacle courses, or just messing around with friends, there’s always something new to try. The game can be challenging at first, but once you get the hang of the movement, it becomes super satisfying. I’d rate Grab VR an 10/10 for creativity, replayability, and pure fun.

Grab vr is honestly one of the most fun games I’ve played in a while. The gameplay is smooth, the graphics look great, and there’s always something exciting happening that keeps me coming back. I really like how the game balances action and strategy without making it feel too complicated. The controls are easy to learn, and the overall design feels polished and well made.

What impressed me the most is how addictive and entertaining it is even after playing for hours. You can tell the developers put a lot of effort into the details and making the experience enjoyable for players. Definitely deserves 5 stars and I can’t wait to see future updates!

I really like grab, it’s just so fun to build and experiment! It’s nice to just make some cool things with people in a free build but also exciting to make a challenging map. This game is one of the best vr games out there and is like the VR version of Roblox. I think that if they added some features for more exact experience and maybe more understandable blocks (most players are kids and can’t code) the game would be its own game engine. It’s just great all around, (kind of) nice community, great levels, shocking complexity, and I haven’t gotten bored since my first oculus account all the way back from 2023. All the top levels really feel like they aren’t even made in the same game, and it feels great. I love this game. Go play it!

Grab is honestly one of my favorite games. I started in 2023 and loved it ever since. As of today, (March 12, 2026) I had some good update Ideas. Most of them came to the game, others didn’t, and here are my ideas:

-Liquids. They flow like water and can behave like water. They can be either creamy like maple syrup, or pure liquid like water. You will be able to change the color and stiffness on the Selection tab.

-Custom shapes. A new tab on the editor menu that lets you create your own custom shapes for 2 complexity like all shapes. You can use straight lines or free lines.

-Voice to sound. A new feature to the sound block. Speak into your mic when using it to create new sounds which will sound exactly like what you said.

-Pay for Play. People will get paid 1 Grab Coin for each visit on a level. Any visits on a level before this will be added do not count.

-Sound storage. Lets you use sounds published by other people on it. You can also publish and title your sounds.

-Code/Trigger deletion: Code and/or Trigger block can delete objects. The only way to bring them back is in Editor.

-Custom textures. You can make your own textures by drawing them. Using custom textures will double the complexity.

-Multi Color Gradient. The new gradient feature can have more than just 2 colors. Max is 20.

-Animated Physics. Physic blocks can now have animated children. That’s it.

So, I couldn’t think of any more ideas. But please, Nils, please add these. You don’t have to, but it would make me happy if you did.

Awesome movement, good player models and just overall great game play! One thing I ask is to remove all the gooner levels thx grab creators!

Grab  completely blew me away. From the moment I jumped in, the experience felt smooth, immersive, and genuinely fun. The controls are intuitive, the environments are incredibly detailed, and everything just works the way you want it to.

What really stands out is how engaging it is—you don’t just play, you feel like you’re inside the action. Whether you’re exploring, interacting, or just messing around, it keeps you coming back for more.

If you’re even slightly interested in VR, this is a must-try. Easily one of the best VR experiences out there right
